verses.text: God shows 'a basket of summer fruit': 'the end is come upon my people'; the temple songs shall be 'howlings.' Ripe for judgment. Like fruit ready to spoil, the nation is ripe for the end.
God shows 'a basket of summer fruit': 'the end is come upon my people'; the temple songs shall be 'howlings.' Ripe for judgment. Like fruit ready to spoil, the nation is ripe for the end.
God shows 'a basket of summer fruit': 'the end is come upon my people'; the temple songs shall be 'howlings.' Ripe for judgment. Like fruit ready to spoil, the nation is ripe for the end.
Hear, ye who 'swallow up the needy,' making 'the ephah small, and the shekel great,' selling the refuse of the wheat. Cheating the poor. God watches the dishonest scales of the greedy.
Hear, ye who 'swallow up the needy,' making 'the ephah small, and the shekel great,' selling the refuse of the wheat. Cheating the poor. God watches the dishonest scales of the greedy.
Hear, ye who 'swallow up the needy,' making 'the ephah small, and the shekel great,' selling the refuse of the wheat. Cheating the poor. God watches the dishonest scales of the greedy.
The LORD has sworn He will 'never forget any of their works'; shall not the land tremble? Nothing forgotten. God remembers every wrong done to the helpless.
The LORD has sworn He will 'never forget any of their works'; shall not the land tremble? Nothing forgotten. God remembers every wrong done to the helpless.
He will darken the sun at noon and 'turn your feasts into mourning,' like grief for an only son. Joy reversed. The day of mirth becomes a day of bitter sorrow.
He will darken the sun at noon and 'turn your feasts into mourning,' like grief for an only son. Joy reversed. The day of mirth becomes a day of bitter sorrow.
'I will send a famine in the land, not... of bread... but of hearing the words of the LORD'; they shall seek it and not find. Worst famine. To lose access to God's word is the direst want of all.
'I will send a famine in the land, not... of bread... but of hearing the words of the LORD'; they shall seek it and not find. Worst famine. To lose access to God's word is the direst want of all.
The fair and strong shall faint for thirst; those who swear by Samaria's idols shall fall. Idols fail. The gods men swore by cannot uphold them in the day of need.
The fair and strong shall faint for thirst; those who swear by Samaria's idols shall fall. Idols fail. The gods men swore by cannot uphold them in the day of need.
